title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: Tiny probes make sense of abnormal bursts in the epileptic brain
items.description: “Spikes” hijack neurons involved in cognition—and can be predicted up to 1 second in advance

items.title: AI helps create bacterium that’s partially missing a universal amino acid
items.description: Advance could suggest new ways to synthesize proteins with bespoke functions in medicine and biotechnology

items.title: AI is starting to beat doctors at making correct diagnoses
items.description: Large language model excels at clinical decisions, even in fast pace of a simulated ER

items.title: Deepfakes are everywhere. The godfather of digital forensics is fighting back
items.description: Hany Farid, who's spent his career building tools to detect fake images, is facing his biggest challenge yet: AI

items.title: Measles explodes in Bangladesh after vaccination breakdown, killing hundreds of children
items.description: Changes to government’s vaccine purchase system after 2024 revolution led to nationwide shortages, gaps in immunity

items.title: After outcry, European funder reverses abrupt rule change restricting resubmissions
items.description: Decision from the European Research Council comes after more than 1000 scientists signed open letter decrying plans

items.title: Guns and bulletproof vests: How federal agents arrested Fauci aide
items.description: David Morens, 78, was stripped and handcuffed for email violations

items.title: To misinformation researchers, AI is a scourge—and a powerful new tool
items.description: Surge in generative AI is upending a field that was just finding its feet

items.title: A novel gene-therapy approach to ‘functionally cure’ HIV succeeds in some monkeys
items.description: Six animals suppressed AIDS virus after one shot of treatment that makes cell-receptor antibody

items.title: House spending panel bumps up Trump’s request for NSF and NASA
items.description: Draft bill is an early sign that Congress again thinks Trump has gone too far in cutting research
